articles.title: Maple syrup and daffodils show signs of spring around New England
articles.description:
Readers weigh in on pajamas at Logan Airport, plus a Vermont ski wedding, and the story behind the Berkshires’ colorful winter icicles.

The post Maple syrup and daffodils show signs of spring around New England appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Maple syrup production is ramping up in New England as spring approaches.
Daffodils are starting to bloom, signaling seasonal changes for local gardeners.
Residents can enjoy outdoor activities as warmer weather arrives in Boston.

articles.title: Fundraiser launched for family of Southborough crash victim, beloved local bartender
articles.description:
A GoFundMe campaign is raising money for the family of a Massachusetts bartender remembered for her generosity and resilience after surviving breast cancer.

The post Fundraiser launched for family of Southborough crash victim, beloved local bartender appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: GoFundMe campaign launched for family of Southborough bartender killed in a crash.
Bartender, known for her generosity, survived breast cancer before her tragic death.
Funds raised will support her family during this difficult time in the community.

articles.title: Jayson Tatum: Active for the game, never questionable in the tunnel
articles.description:
After 10 months of wondering and waiting, fans will see Jayson Tatum make the return to the TD Garden floor and the NBA fashion tunnel as an active player.

The post Jayson Tatum: Active for the game, never questionable in the tunnel appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Jayson Tatum returns to TD Garden as an active player after 10 months.
Fans can see Tatum in the NBA fashion tunnel starting March 6, 2026.
His return boosts excitement and morale for Boston Celtics fans this season.

articles.title: How Susan Hurley’s legacy inspired her son to run the Boston Marathon
articles.description:
Max McGillivray is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of his mother, Susan Hurley, in support of the Justice Health Initiative.

The post How Susan Hurley’s legacy inspired her son to run the Boston Marathon appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Max McGillivray will run the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of Susan Hurley.
He supports the Justice Health Initiative, promoting health equity in Boston.
The marathon run aims to raise awareness and funds for local health initiatives.

articles.title: Back Bay’s ‘ugly duckling’ is getting a makeover
articles.description:
Developers are transforming a Commonwealth Avenue apartment building into a luxury single-family home overlooking the Alexander Hamilton monument.

The post Back Bay’s ‘ugly duckling’ is getting a makeover appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Developers are converting a Commonwealth Avenue apartment into a luxury single-family home.
The project is located near the Alexander Hamilton monument in Back Bay.
Residents can expect increased property values and neighborhood revitalization from this transformation.

articles.title: Country star Bailey Zimmerman surprises Boston bar crowd, picks up everyone’s tab
articles.description:
After performing a sold-out show at TD Garden, the country singer stopped by a South End pub.

The post Country star Bailey Zimmerman surprises Boston bar crowd, picks up everyone’s tab appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Country star Bailey Zimmerman surprised patrons at a South End pub after his TD Garden show.
Zimmerman covered the tab for everyone in the bar, enhancing the local nightlife experience.
This gesture fosters community spirit and supports local businesses in Boston's South End.

articles.title: Haitian man living in Boston dies in ICE custody, heightening concerns about detention centers
articles.description:
Emmanuel Damas died after health care workers in an ICE center run by a for-profit prison company did not adequately treat a toothache, according to his family.

The post Haitian man living in Boston dies in ICE custody, heightening concerns about detention centers appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Emmanuel Damas, a Haitian man, died in ICE custody on March 6, 2026.
His family claims inadequate treatment for a toothache contributed to his death.
The incident raises concerns about health care standards in Boston's detention centers.

articles.title: Celtics officially list Jayson Tatum as ‘Available’ for game vs. Mavericks
articles.description:
Tatum has not played since rupturing his Achilles tendon in 2025.

The post Celtics officially list Jayson Tatum as ‘Available’ for game vs. Mavericks appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Celtics list Jayson Tatum as 'Available' for March 7 game against Mavericks.
Tatum has been recovering since rupturing his Achilles tendon in 2025.
His return boosts Celtics' chances for playoff success in Boston.

articles.title: Patriots reportedly agree to two-year deal with backup QB Tommy DeVito
articles.description:
DeVito was a New England backup behind Drake Maye in 2025.

The post Patriots reportedly agree to two-year deal with backup QB Tommy DeVito appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Patriots sign backup QB Tommy DeVito to a two-year contract.
DeVito previously backed up Drake Maye in 2025 season.
Local fans anticipate increased competition for quarterback position.
